reasoning: The base rate of 'Riots' in Hungary over the preceding 360 days is recorded by ACLED as practically zero (producing a reference value of 1.000). To trigger a YES resolution, there must be strictly more than 10 (i.e. 11 or more) riots within the 30 days prior to the resolution date. While there are ongoing anti-government protests in Hungary regarding anti-LGBT legislation and the upcoming 2026 elections, recent demonstrations have been entirely peaceful and have not involved violence or destruction as required by ACLED's 'Riots' definition. For the 2025-11-16 resolution date, 23 of the 30 days have already passed with zero recorded riots, making 11+ events before the deadline exceptionally improbable. For the later deadlines (2025-12-09 and 2026-02-07), getting an unprecedented 11+ riots in a single 30-day period without a preexisting escalation of violence is highly unlikely. However, due to the longer forecast horizon and the approaching 2026 parliamentary elections, uncertainty slightly increases for the furthest date. Probabilities are therefore kept at the minimum allowed limit (0.05) and slightly stepped up to 0.06 for the last horizon.
